ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evyn Shomer appeared by zoom for San Francisco Public Guardian (Trustee).

The Court noted that this matter is on calendar for it to inquire about status of any plans to make disbursements to enrich Mr. O'Neill's life. Ms. Shomer stated that she spoke with Jeffrey Price (assigned Deputy Public Guardian), that Mr. Price contacted the Director at Mr. O'Neill's placement, that she filed a declaration, and that they came up with several options to purchase items and services to enhance Mr. O'Neill's quality of living. Ms. Shomer also stated that upon funding of the trust Mr. O'Neill was able to be moved to a private room, that the plan is to make purchases and putting services in place to further benefit Mr. O'Neill and that several of the items have already been ordered. The Second Account and Report, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (60 Westwood Dr. San Francisco, Ca 94112)
Off Calendar Apr-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Evyn Shomer for and with Jeffrey Price (San Francisco Deputy Public Guardian)
Kelly O'Neill (Daughter)
Adam LaPointe (Daughter's Fiance)

Ms. Shomer stated that she received the objection yesterday, has discussed the objection with her client, understands Ms. O'Neill's disappointment and preference not to sell, but the objection is too late. Ms. Shomer also stated that Mr. O'Neill's estate has $65,000 in arrearages at his placement/board and care facility, the estate cannot bear to maintain the property, the estate is exhausted and only has $1,200 in it, and a petition for authority to sell the house was granted in September 2017 in the Conservatorship case. In addition, Ms. Shomer stated that Mr. O'Neill's income is needed for the payment of the board and care, that the petition to sell was with the agreement of Gemma O'Keefe (Court Appointed Counsel for Mr. O'Neill), it is necessary for the sale to proceed/go forward. Ms. O'Neill and Mr. LaPointe presented oral argument to support their positions. Mr. LaPointe placed a first overbid, but did not have a cashier's check for 10% as required. The sale was confirmed at $1,250,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed on April 2, 2018. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No bond is required.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
